title: XRN1

(**Xrn1**) is a [protein](/wiki/Protein "Protein") that in humans is encoded by the XRN1 [gene](/wiki/Gene "Gene"). Xrn1 [hydrolyses](/wiki/Hydrolysis "Hydrolysis") [RNA](/wiki/RNA "RNA") in the 5′ to 3′ [direction](/wiki/Directionality_(molecular_biology) "Directionality (molecular biology)").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-entrez-6)

**5′-3′ exoribonuclease 1** (**Xrn1**) is a [protein](/wiki/Protein "Protein") that in humans is encoded by the XRN1 [gene](/wiki/Gene "Gene"). Xrn1 [hydrolyses](/wiki/Hydrolysis "Hydrolysis") [RNA](/wiki/RNA "RNA") in the 5′ to 3′ [direction](/wiki/Directionality_(molecular_biology) "Directionality (molecular biology)").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-entrez-6)

## Function

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=XRN1_(gene)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Function")]

This gene encodes a member of the 5′-3′ [exonuclease](/wiki/Exonuclease "Exonuclease") family. The encoded protein may be involved in replication-dependent [histone](/wiki/Histone "Histone") [mRNA](/wiki/Messenger_RNA "Messenger RNA") degradation, and interacts directly with the enhancer of [mRNA-decapping](/wiki/Messenger_RNA_decapping "Messenger RNA decapping") protein 4. In addition to mRNA metabolism, a similar protein in yeast has been implicated in a variety of nuclear and cytoplasmic functions, including [transcription](/wiki/Eukaryotic_transcription "Eukaryotic transcription"), [translation](/wiki/Eukaryotic_translation "Eukaryotic translation"), [homologous recombination](/wiki/Homologous_recombination "Homologous recombination"), [meiosis](/wiki/Meiosis "Meiosis"), [telomere](/wiki/Telomere "Telomere") maintenance, and [microtubule](/wiki/Microtubule "Microtubule") assembly. Mutations in this gene are associated with [osteosarcoma](/wiki/Osteosarcoma "Osteosarcoma"), suggesting that the encoded protein may also play a role in [bone formation](/wiki/Bone_formation "Bone formation"). [Alternative splicing](/wiki/Alternative_splicing "Alternative splicing") results in multiple transcript variants.

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=XRN1_(gene)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Xrn2](/wiki/5%27-3%27_exoribonuclease_2 "5'-3' exoribonuclease 2")
